title: Move all python tests to pytest
body:
* Add configuration to add report header for python client used in tests

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body: I re-ran apiv2, some timeout error, assuming it's a flake.

> I re-ran apiv2, some timeout error, assuming it's a flake.

Yeah, I also beefed up the error message so I could debug which service  timed out

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@jwhonce I'm not sure where you're at with this, but just FYI: If you're trying to debug only one or two tasks, you can cycle ***much*** faster by temporarily skipping nearly everything else.  The only tasks which are nearly always required is `build for XYZ`.  Everything else you can temporarily mark with `skip: $CI == $CI` (I know it's weird).  Just blast that into `.cirrus.yml` after any existing `skip` line, and it will rip right through and run only the tasks you're interested in.

Good to know. I found what is happening and added comments to be addressed later.
I'm assuming this is ready, but set `/hold` just in case it's not.  Please cancel hold if appropriate.
